Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack (#customer-engagement or broad) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "PathFinder" or "retention") to discover Pam's escalation over Catalina's head, the PathFinder competitive threat in Denver, and team coordination on the NorthWind churn crisis.
OE 2: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "chain-of-custody") to discover Pam's direct escalation email to David, the chain-of-custody breach details from Raj Patel's server move, and any prior promises David made to Pam.
OE 3: Search Slack (#finance or broad) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "NorthWind discount" or "concession" or "Alejandro" or "retention") to discover Marcus's NorthWind pricing analysis flagging NorthWind as "expensive and fragile," Alejandro's Q1 margin analysis, and INV-2026-0412 chain-of-custody provisional charges, providing financial context for the retention proposal.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` (criteria: { "CustomerRef": "NorthWind" }) to discover INV-2026-003 ($60,000 for 5 enterprise relocations at $12,000 each) and the chain-of-custody $1,200 PROVISIONAL line item.
OE 5: Search CRM using `crm_search_deals` (dealname: "NorthWind") or `crm_search_companies` (name: "NorthWind") to retrieve NorthWind deal history, renewal status, and revenue data for the retention proposal.
OE 6: Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` (query: "NorthWind retention") to discover linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (NorthWind retention response plan), which is the internally tracked retention plan David references.
OE 7: Retrieve Linear comments using `linear_list_comments` (issueId: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) to discover Alejandro's working draft proposing a 12% Q3 discount conditional on NorthWind committing full Denver expansion volume, noted as incomplete and not ready to socialize.
OE 8: Look up Pam Kowalski's contact info using `crm_search_contacts` (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") to retrieve her email address pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com for sending the retention proposal. (Note: Pam's contact record is in CRM contacts, not the Contacts service. Her email is also discoverable from OE 2's email search results.)
OE 9: Send retention proposal email to Pam Kowalski using `send_email` (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) with content that owns what went wrong (chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server move, Emilia Cruz's piano damage) and proposes a concrete financial concession.
OE 10: Add a comment to the NorthWind retention Linear issue using `linear_create_comment` (issueId: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) summarizing investigation findings and updating the retention plan status so the internal tracking reflects what was discovered.
OE 11: Search Slack (#operations or #customer-support) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Simone" or "goodwill credit") to reconstruct the full picture of unresolved StormCloud items from the original three relocations.
OE 12: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "StormCloud" or "Liam Park" or "escalation") to find Liam Park's escalation details and any prior communications about unresolved relocation items.
OE 13: Search Slack (#finance) using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "StormCloud credit" or "EST-2026-0287" or "INV-2026-0319" or "goodwill") to discover Marcus's flag that the $500 goodwill credit was posted to the cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice INV-2026-0319.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` (criteria: { "CustomerRef": "StormCloud" }) to retrieve StormCloud billing history, including INV-2026-002 ($26,400 for 3 Premium Relocation Packages). Note: the $500 credit mispost details (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319) are discovered via Slack #finance in OE 13, not in QuickBooks records directly.
OE 15: Search Linear using `linear_list_issues` (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won") to discover linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 (#412, Jae-won lease) and linear_issue_a49b4d199139 (StormCloud incident reconstruction) for open ticket tracking.
OE 16: Look up Liam Park's contact info using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Liam Park") to retrieve his email address liam.park@stormcloud.io for sending the remediation plan.
OE 17: Send remediation plan email to Liam Park using `send_email` (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: liam.park@stormcloud.io) with content covering Jae-won Kim's unresolved lease status, Simone Richter's frozen stipend, the $500 credit correction plan, and a timeline for resolution.
OE 18: Post a message to the #operations Slack channel using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C006) flagging outstanding StormCloud items (Jae-won lease, Simone stipend, credit correction) so the ops team knows what's still stuck.
OE 19: Search emails using `search_emails` (query: "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "Series B" or "expansion") to find Tessa's expansion request and Mina's audit findings about open issues in the April batch.
OE 20: Search Slack using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "BrightLoop" or "Mina audit" or "April batch" or "open issues") to discover Mina's findings that the April batch isn't clean: Simone Richter got the wrong unit type and Marcus Webb's car shipment is delayed.
OE 21: Search Airtable using `airtable_search_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01", field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop") or `airtable_list_records` (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "tblRelocations01") to retrieve current BrightLoop relocation status and assess ops capacity for six additional May relocations.
OE 22: Search QuickBooks using `quickbooks_search_invoices` (criteria: { "CustomerRef": "BrightLoop" }) to discover INV-2026-0305 ($10K), Simone Richter's billing ($4,500 + $750 rush fee), and Marcus Webb's billing ($4,500 + $1,100 vehicle shipping).
OE 23: Search CRM using `crm_search_deals` (dealname: "BrightLoop") or `crm_search_companies` (name: "BrightLoop") to retrieve BrightLoop company and deal info for logging the expansion outreach engagement.
OE 24: Look up Tessa Moreno's contact info using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Tessa Moreno") to retrieve her email address tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com for the expansion confirmation email.
OE 25: Send expansion confirmation email to Tessa Moreno using `send_email` (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) acknowledging open April items (Simone's unit issue, Marcus's car delay) and confirming readiness for six May expansion relocations.
OE 26: Create a CRM engagement using `crm_create_engagement` (engagement_type: EMAIL or NOTE, contact_ids: [Tessa Moreno], company_ids: [company_brightloop]) logging the BrightLoop expansion outreach so Mina has a record.
OE 27: Post a summary message to the #customer-engagement Slack channel using `conversations_add_message` (channel_id: C002) covering the status of all three accounts: NorthWind retention proposal sent, StormCloud remediation plan delivered, BrightLoop expansion confirmed, so the customer engagement team has visibility across the board.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
